items.title: Top Somali official deported from Kenya over suspected passport fraud
items.description: Somalia’s Second Deputy Prime Minister was deported from Kenya on Thursday over allegations of passport fraud, according to numerous reports.
items.titleIt: Top Somali official deported from Kenya over suspected passport fraud

items.title: Somalia: Somalia Deputy Prime Minister Deported From Kenya Over Alleged Passport Fraud
items.description: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Somalia's Deputy Prime Minister Jibril Abdirashid Haji was deported after he was allegedly found in possession of a fraudulently acquired Kenyan passport at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport (JKIA), according to a police incident report.
items.titleIt: Somalia: Somalia Deputy Prime Minister Deported From Kenya Over Alleged Passport Fraud

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Sudan, South Sudan and Somalia: Key Peace and Security Priorities for Africa in 2026
items.description: In this online event, Crisis Group experts discussed peace and security priorities for the African continent in 2026, zooming in on Somalia, Sudan and South Sudan ahead of the African Union's summit of heads of state.
items.titleIt: Sudan, South Sudan and Somalia: Key Peace and Security Priorities for Africa in 2026

items.title: Africa: Ocean Economy Can Drive Africa's Future If Backed by Bold Investment
items.description: [allAfrica] Mombasa, Kenya -- The blue economy is a vital source of jobs, food security, and economic opportunity across Africa and the Western Indian Ocean region. Yet, by the admission of those driving its development, the region is still only beginning to tap into what its oceans, lakes, and rivers can truly offer.
items.titleIt: Africa: Ocean Economy Can Drive Africa's Future If Backed by Bold Investment

items.title: Somalia: Somaliland Marks 66th Independence, Reaffirms Sovereignty
items.description: [Horn Diplomat] Hargeisa -- Somaliland celebrated the 66th anniversary of its independence from Britain on Thursday, with President Abdirahman Mohamed Abdullahi Irro reaffirming the country's sovereignty, national unity and commitment to democratic statehood during a state ceremony in Hargeisa.
items.titleIt: Somalia: Somaliland Marks 66th Independence, Reaffirms Sovereignty

items.title: Soccer: Nico Paz to stay at Como
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 26 - 21-year-old Argentina attacking midfielder Nico Paz is to stay on loan at Serie A club Como after they reached an agreement with his owner Real Madrid, the Lariani said Friday. The news, circulating from Spain, had been in the air, but an agreement between the Como club and Real Madrid was reportedly reached in the last few hours. The Spanish club will, however, maintain control over the player, with a buy-back clause of €80 million. The overall deal involves approximately €60 million for Como, which aims to make the buy-back clause valid only for the next summer transf
items.titleIt: Soccer: Nico Paz to stay at Como

The news, circulating from Spain, had been in the air, but an agreement between the Como club and Real Madrid was reportedly reached in the last few hours. The overall deal involves approximately €60 million for Como, which aims to make the buy-back clause valid only for the next summer transf
items.titleIt: Soccer: Nico Paz to stay at Como

items.title: Ethiopia: Ethiopia's Digital Revolution - From Connectivity to Competitiveness
items.description: [ENA] Across Africa, nations are embracing the digital revolution as a catalyst for economic growth, stronger governance, and greater global competitiveness. Among them, Ethiopia--long celebrated for its ancient civilization, rich cultural heritage, and strategic position in the Horn of Africa, is emerging as one of the continent's most ambitious digital transformation stories.
items.titleIt: Ethiopia: Ethiopia's Digital Revolution - From Connectivity to Competitiveness

items.title: Mexico Celebrates the Third “Made in Italy” Day
items.description: (ANSA) - CITTÀ DEL MESSICO, 25 GIU - The third edition of the National “Made in Italy” Day, titled “Italian Technological Excellence in Mexico in Strategic Sectors,” was held in the Mexican capital. The event was dedicated to highlighting the technological and industrial contributions of Italian companies in Mexico. The Italian Ambassador, Alessandro Modiano, confirmed the strength of trade ties, noting that “the main sector for Italian exports to Mexico is industrial machinery, which accounts for nearly 40% of the total.” Director Gravela highlighted the ICE Agency’s commitment to promoting I
items.titleIt: Mexico Celebrates the Third “Made in Italy” Day

items.title: As war keeps spreading, the African Union must find its teeth
items.description: With the continental body at its weakest point since its inception, this week’s summit in Ethiopia cannot be another talk shop. A united front is crucial to resolve intensifying conflicts tearing through Africa.
items.titleIt: As war keeps spreading, the African Union must find its teeth
